title: Post-operative eye wound review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting expected visual changes, possible complication symptoms, activity or driving restrictions, contact arrangements, next appointment, vision change, spectacles, second-eye discussion, visual function, quality of life, and postoperative review processes without mapping medicines, doses, or autonomous urgency.
assessment.rationale: Supports postoperative cataract review, complications and follow-up context for post-operative eye wound review; no decision is generated.

source.title: Cataracts in adults: management
source.population: Adults aged 18 years and over with cataracts, including people considering referral or surgery and people receiving postoperative review.
source.setting: Primary, optometric, ophthalmology, surgical, and postoperative cataract care.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected cataract identity, vision and quality-of-life impact, laterality, preference-sensitive surgery discussion, postoperative information, visual-function review, and clinician-arranged follow-up fields. It does not support autonomous diagnosis, surgery or referral, medicine, dose, fixed review timing, or UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3 — postoperative information and assessment
exact_location.locator: recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3; official page lines 101–136 and 473–489

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting expected visual changes, possible complication symptoms, activity or driving restrictions, contact arrangements, next appointment, vision change, spectacles, second-eye discussion, visual function, quality of life, and postoperative review processes without mapping medicines, doses, or autonomous urgency.

assessment.text: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.
assessment.rationale: Supports general surgical-site infection recognition context for post-operative eye wound review; no decision is generated.

source.title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations
source.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
source.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
source.exclusions: Exact for postoperative wound-care and surgical-site-infection documentation; partial for traumatic wounds, suture removal, and UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

sources_used.title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations
